seoKeywords: how much solar do I need
explanation:
The home solar sizing calculator is based on proven efficiency standards and industry best practices. We apply standard formulas with your inputs, checking realistic ranges and alerting you to outliers.

## How It's Calculated

Your required solar panel capacity depends on your monthly electricity consumption, location's peak sun hours, and system efficiency. This calculator uses industry-standard formulas to estimate the kW capacity your home needs for self-consumption.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for planning and educational purposes. For critical decisions—financing, installation, warranty claims—consult a qualified solar installer. Data is current for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How do I calculate what size solar system I need?
faq.a: Divide your monthly electricity consumption (kWh) by your area's average peak sun hours per day, then adjust for system losses. This calculator does that automatically.

faq.q: What size solar system do I need for 1000 kWh per month?
faq.a: It depends on your location. In areas with 5 peak sun hours daily and 80% system efficiency, you'd need roughly 3–4 kW. Northern regions need larger systems.

faq.q: What's the difference between kW and kWh?
faq.a: kW is your solar system's power rating (instant capacity). kWh is energy produced over time. A 5 kW system might generate 20–25 kWh on a sunny day.
Within ±10–15% for most residential setups. Real output varies by season, weather, roof shading, temperature, and equipment degradation. Always consult an installer.

faq.q: What factors affect my solar panel sizing?
faq.a: Monthly consumption, latitude (affects peak sun hours), roof orientation, shading from trees/buildings, climate, seasonal patterns, and system efficiency losses all matter.
Yes, completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: Why do different regions have different peak sun hours?
faq.a: Peak sun hours vary by latitude, climate, and seasonal weather. Arizona gets 5–6 hours daily; northern states get 3–4. Your location's solar resource directly affects sizing.

faq.q: Do I need to account for battery storage in this calculation?
faq.a: This calculates solar panel generation only. If you want backup batteries, you'll need additional capacity. Discuss off-grid vs. grid-tied with your solar installer.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
